behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: model-judgment seam at diarization/speaker labeling quality in STT output. A stubbed STT response would hide real labeling errors (MOCK-HIDE), so real model output is required (LLM-DEP/AI-DEP). I considered Workflow because the text mentions summary attribution, but rejected it: the behavior explicitly targets "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels"; attribution can be validated from that output without requiring a full multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the real STT API and assert transcript chunks include correct, stable speaker labels tied to known attendees; then verify those labels are present in the handoff payload used by summarization.

behaviors.description: The structured summary contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: model-judgment seam at LLM extraction/completeness of action items and assignee mapping. Deterministic mocks can always return perfect coverage and miss real omission failures (MOCK-HIDE). Integration brake: no real OS/subprocess need, but correctness depends on real model output, so Integration is too low. I considered Workflow and rejected it because this behavior can be tested with a fixed transcript directly into summarization (single agentic step).
behaviors.test_description: Provide a transcript with known task assignments to multiple attendees to the real summarization LLM and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in structured output.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the "decisions" section of the summary, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: model-judgment seam for semantic extraction and section placement in generated structure. Mocked summaries would not expose misclassification/placement errors (MOCK-HIDE). Integration brake: no mandatory real OS/subprocess boundary, but real AI output quality is the core risk, so Integration is insufficient. Workflow was considered and rejected because behavior text scopes to one summary output contract, not multi-step orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Send transcripts containing explicit decision statements to the real LLM summarizer and assert those decisions are present in the dedicated "decisions" field/section and not only in free-form narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: multi-step orchestration seam (segmenting, repeated transcribe/summarize passes, ordered processing, merge). This is explicitly multiple agentic steps in sequence (MULTI-STEP), and mocks can hide cross-segment coherence/regression issues. I considered Agentic-only, but rejected it because behavior text requires sequential segment processing plus merged final coherence, which is orchestration across repeated agentic calls.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording, execute production segmentation and sequential processing against real STT/LLM services, and assert ordered segment handling, complete segment coverage, and a final merged summary that preserves cross-segment continuity (no dropped/duplicated major items).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output — completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: full multi-step orchestration seam across environment + multiple agentic stages end-to-end. Behavior explicitly names complete pipeline with real recording, crossing upload/processing/delivery boundaries and chained model calls; lower levels would miss handoff and long-path failures. Rejection-gate: System alone is disqualified by behavior text "transcribe, summarize" plus full pipeline sequencing, which requires multiple agentic steps, not just infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end production-like job with a real meeting audio file from upload through output delivery, using real external services, and assert terminal success state, structured schema validity, and no human retries/interventions during execution.
